channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: What’s new across Microsoft SQL in 2026 so far (SQL Server, Azure SQL, and SQL database in Fabric)
item.description: We’re halfway through 2026, and Microsoft SQL has not slowed down. Since SQLCon/FabCon in March (where we released a ton of things, and those updates can be found in this udpates video), we shipped a wave of updates across SQL Server, Azure SQL, and SQL database in Fabric, with Microsoft Build 2026...
item.dc:creator: Anna Hoffman

item.title: Cost Savings In Microsoft 365
item.description: Long story short, I had a client say, "We have more M365 licensed users than employees. Please help us find them."It took me about a week to find all the waste (first time always takes forever). Now I've run the same steps for a dozen other clients (takes less than an hour now). On average, I've...
item.dc:creator: John Gruber

item.title: Supercharge your real-time data ingestion: What's new in Fabric Eventstream connectors
item.description: Discover the latest Fabric Eventstream connector updates—bringing stronger enterprise security, broader source coverage, and simpler real-time ingestion across private networks, Kafka, Service Bus, IoT Hub, Oracle CDC, and HTTP.Now available: Connector private network support, Apache Kafka...
item.dc:creator: xujx

item.title: Announcing Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (2027 Edition)
item.description: The editorial team is happy to announce the publication of Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (2027 edition) eBook, the most comprehensive and up-to-date book covering tenant management. Previously named Office 365 for IT Pros and now spanning four books with the addition of Microsoft Purview for IT Pros...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Smoke Test Microsoft Foundry Agents with GitHub Actions
item.description: Introduction This blog is the next part of a series discussing Foundry Hosted Agents and how to properly construct Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) for them. This post will specifically go over configuring smoke tests against your recently deployed hosted agents. You can...
item.dc:creator: j_folberth

item.title: Shaping Software While It Runs: A Canvas Scenario, Start to Finish
item.description: There is a moment, the first time you open a GitHub Copilot App Canvas, when you reach for the wrong metaphor. You see panels, buttons, live status cards and you think "dashboard." You start designing a DevOps board. We did exactly that. Then we watched the recording back, and we all agreed: it was...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Access GitHub Copilot in JetBrains – GitHub Copilot Is Now a Native Agent in JetBrains AI Assistant
item.description: GitHub Copilot is now a first-class, native option in JetBrains AI Assistant agent picker(read more). You can select it directly from the chat interface, no extra configuration needed, and immediately put Copilot to work on real coding tasks without ever leaving AI Assistant. Millions of developers...
item.dc:creator: Hang Wang, Antonio Goncalves, Jun Han

item.title: SharePoint Framework (SPFx) roadmap update – July 2026
item.description: June celebrates the announcement of upcoming SharePoint Copilot Apps, ships the quality-focused SPFx 1.23.2 release, and shares the roadmap for the AI era - shaped by your feedback. The post SharePoint Framework (SPFx) roadmap update – July 2026 appeared first on Microsoft 365 Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Vesa Juvonen

item.title: Azure Container Apps Express for Shipping Container Apps Fast
item.description: ACA Express Apps are a strong fit for teams that need to ship quickly and can't afford long platform setup cycles. This includes startups, internal platform teams, and product groups deploying APIs, web apps, or agent endpoints that scale with uneven demand. If the priority is fast...
item.dc:creator: hetvip

item.title: Compose your API surface with Data API builder custom paths
item.description: Data API builder (DAB) 2.0 adds compound paths for REST endpoints, giving developers more control over how their API surface is organized. Instead of mirroring database topology, endpoints can now reflect simple names, business areas, or schema ownership. The post Compose your API surface with Data...
item.dc:creator: Jerry Nixon

item.title: Item Recovery in Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available)
item.description: With Item Recovery in Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available), you can now recover deleted items across supported workloads, restore items through the Workspace Recycle bin or REST API, and configure tenant-wide recovery policies.
item.dc:creator: YuliaTurchin

item.title: Accelerating the quantum-safe timeline
item.description: We’re accelerating quantum-safe readiness—and sharing what organizations can do now to transition earlier and with confidence. The post Accelerating the quantum-safe timeline appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Mark Russinovich

item.title: Power BI Desktop Bridge (Preview)
item.description: Power BI Desktop Bridge (Preview)Power BI Desktop Bridge introduces a new way of working, allowing external applications and agents to interact directly with Power BI Desktop! What is Power BI Desktop Bridge?The bridge is a lightweight local server that lets external applications interact directly...
item.dc:creator: saralam

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud expands multicloud coverage across AWS and Google Cloud
item.description: Organizations are building and running applications across multiple cloud platforms and hybrid environments to move faster, improve resilience, and choose the services that best fit each workload. But that flexibility also changes how teams need to manage exposure. A risk may start with an...
item.dc:creator: donnalee

item.title: How GitHub maintains compliance for open source dependencies
item.description: Explore how the Open Source Program Office uses GitHub’s new license compliance product to manage open source dependencies at scale. The post How GitHub maintains compliance for open source dependencies appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Claude Sonnet 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Claude Sonnet 5 is Anthropic’s latest Sonnet-class model, now available in GitHub Copilot. It brings strong coding performance to everyday development and agentic workflows, giving developers a new Sonnet-class option… The post Claude Sonnet 5 is generally available for GitHub Copilot appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: AI-assisted Synapse Spark and pipeline migration to Microsoft Fabric from the command line (Preview)
item.description: Many organizations running Apache Spark workloads on Azure Synapse Analytics are planning their move to Microsoft Fabric for a more unified analytics experience. This preview introduces an AI-assisted migration skill that helps move Synapse Spark artifacts to Fabric from the command line, using...
item.dc:creator: jiang_jenny1

item.title: MCP Beyond the Chat Window: Build Diagnostics in CI
item.description: A practical tour of the Model Context Protocol tools for .NET build diagnostics - the full Binlog MCP toolset, how those tools run inside a GitHub Actions workflow, and what the evaluation data says about the efficiency gains. The post MCP Beyond the Chat Window: Build Diagnostics in CI appeared...
item.dc:creator: Jan Krivanek, Yuliia Kovalova

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: June 2026
item.description: Welcome back to What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. In June, Sentinel SIEM’s Advanced Security Information Model (ASIM) broadens its normalization, so one analytic rule can reach more sources with less per-source work and, additionally, two new ASIM schemas can now bring asset inventory and AI agent...
item.dc:creator: TomerBrand

item.title: GitHub code coverage merge protection for pull requests
item.description: You can now use branch rulesets to block pull requests from merging when test coverage drops below thresholds you set. You can set a minimum coverage percentage, a maximum allowed… The post GitHub code coverage merge protection for pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Releases: Sidebar navigation and per-asset download counts
item.description: You can now scan and navigate release pages more easily with a dedicated sidebar table of contents. We also updated release metadata placement for a more consistent layout so it’s… The post Releases: Sidebar navigation and per-asset download counts appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Visual Studio June Update – Track Your Usage, Trust Your Tools
item.description: The June update includes a Copilot Usage window refresh with proactive alerts as you approach your limits, MCP servers now get a trust check before they run anything new. The post Visual Studio June Update – Track Your Usage, Trust Your Tools appeared first on Visual Studio Blog.
item.dc:creator: Mark Downie

item.title: Copilot Agent is now available in JetBrains AI Assistant
item.description: Today, JetBrains and GitHub are announcing a deeper integration between JetBrains AI Assistant and GitHub Copilot. Millions of developers already rely on the GitHub Copilot plugin as their AI pair… The post Copilot Agent is now available in JetBrains AI Assistant appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Open source license compliance is in public preview
item.description: Enterprises can now manage their dependencies’ licenses at scale with sophisticated, ruleset-based checks that enforce a centralized policy. Open source license compliance is in public preview, letting you block noncompliant… The post Open source license compliance is in public preview appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Behind the Build with Gigamon: Enriching Microsoft Sentinel with Network-Derived Telemetry
item.description: Behind the Build is an ongoing series spotlighting standout Microsoft partner collaborations. Each edition dives into the technical and strategic decisions that shape real-world integrations—highlighting engineering excellence, innovation, and the shared customer value created through partnership....
item.dc:creator: Natee_Pretikul

item.title: The AI-first SOC: Copilot, UEBA, threat intelligence, and SOC optimization
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl The portal change was the easy part. The real upside is the operating model it unlocks—generative AI in every workflow, behavioral analytics across hybrid and multi-cloud, native threat intelligence, and SOC optimization...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1

item.title: ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026
item.description: This month’s updates help security and IT teams strengthen identity and multicloud foundations, protect data wherever it lives, and secure the developer workflows powering AI innovation. The post ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026 appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Alym Rayani

item.title: Securing AI agents: When AI tools move from reading to acting
item.description: MCP tool poisoning turns trusted AI agents into a control plane for data loss. Learn how threat actors manipulate tool descriptions to trigger unauthorized actions, and how to detect, contain, and prevent it. The post Securing AI agents: When AI tools move from reading to acting appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Incident Response

item.title: In the Windows 11 preview, does the system image creation feature still work 100% as expected?
item.description: Microsoft has deprecated the system image feature in the preview version, but the feature still exists. The backup feature works fine, but can it reliably restore data? The new Windows Backup app is too limited in its functionality.
item.dc:creator: AdamWu

item.title: The Windows 11 Preview is prompting me to install a Secure Boot KEK update. Should I install it?
item.description: The system displayed a notification about a Secure Boot KEK update.What is this?Do I need to install it?
item.dc:creator: JulianSanders

item.title: After updating the Insider build, third-party input methods cannot switch input modes properly
item.description: After updating to the latest Insider Preview build, installed third-party input methods cannot switch input modes properly. When attempting to switch, the input method status bar displays incorrectly, and only English can be entered. Neither restarting the input method service nor reinstalling the...
item.dc:creator: Kodyon

item.title: In the Insider Preview, the Settings app often crashes
item.description: In the latest Insider Preview build, the Settings app frequently crashes when opening Settings → System.I have tried clearing the Settings cache, restarting File Explorer, and other methods, but the issue persists, severely affecting system configuration operations.
item.dc:creator: AAbner

item.title: Mailbox requirement set 1.16 now available for Outlook add-ins
item.description: Mailbox requirement set 1.16 is now generally available for Outlook add-ins. This release reflects our continued investment in closing the gap between COM/VSTO and web add-ins, with a focus on message and information security. Mailbox 1.16 introduces APIs and platform updates that enable your...
item.dc:creator: Office Extensibility team

item.title: Dependabot no longer infers .npmrc
item.description: Dependabot will no longer attempt to infer .npmrc configuration for npm private registries. Previously, Dependabot tried to reconstruct .npmrc contents from lockfile resolved URLs, but incorrect lockfile URLs, lockfile format… The post Dependabot no longer infers .npmrc appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Upcoming cloud data retention policy for closed security alerts
item.description: Starting August 25, 2026, GitHub will introduce a data retention policy for closed Dependabot security alerts. This policy gives you a clear commitment for how long your alert data stays… The post Upcoming cloud data retention policy for closed security alerts appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Upcoming access restrictions to public API endpoints and UI views
item.description: As part of our ongoing commitment to protect our users and ensure responsible use of our platform, the Notifications team will soon introduce access restrictions to several public API endpoints… The post Upcoming access restrictions to public API endpoints and UI views appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Sage Cloud Hosting Providers: How to Choose the Right One
item.description: Finding the right Sage cloud hosting provider is about more than moving your accounting software to the cloud. The ideal provider should deliver secure access, reliable performance, and expert support while ensuring your Sage environment remains available whenever your team needs it. Whether you...
item.dc:creator: DavisMiller29

item.title: Using the Small Window for Incoming Teams Calls
item.description: A recent improvement is the introduction of a small window for incoming Teams calls. It doesn’t sound like a great step forward, but switching to the small window is a surprisingly useful step. The sad thing is that many users won’t know that the option to switch exists because it’s buried in Teams...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Per-user AI credit budgets available for cost centers
item.description: Enterprise admins can now set a cost center user-level budget: one per-user AI credit budget on a cost center that applies to every individual in it. As membership changes, the… The post Per-user AI credit budgets available for cost centers appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot App - Canvas Is Not a UI Builder
item.description: What if your development environment didn't just help you write code, but helped you observe, steer, and evolve a living system while it runs? That's the shift GitHub Copilot App Canvas represents. Canvas redefines how developers interact with agent-driven software: not by building traditional user...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: The next frontier in endpoint security: Securing local AI agents with Microsoft Defender
item.description: AI agents are now doing real work on the endpoint — reading files, running commands, browsing the web, and acting on behalf of the users they run under. That same power is also what makes them dangerous: agents act on whatever content they take in, and much of it comes from outside the user's...
item.dc:creator: Eitan_Shteinberg

item.title: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026
item.description: .NET 8 and .NET 9 will both reach end of support on November 10, 2026. Learn what this means and how to upgrade to .NET 10 (LTS). The post .NET 8 and .NET 9 will reach End of Support on November 10, 2026 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT)

item.title: SkiaSharp 4.0 is here: announcing the first stable release
item.description: SkiaSharp 4.148.0 is the first stable release of SkiaSharp v4. Learn what is new, why you should be excited, and join the SkiaSharp live event on June 30. The post SkiaSharp 4.0 is here: announcing the first stable release appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Leibowitz

item.title: Announcing General Availability of Client-Side Data Integrity Protections in Azure Blob Storage
item.description: We are excited to announce the General Availability of client-side data integrity in Azure Blob Storage. Blob Storage has long supported integrity validation, starting with MD5 as part of the original HTTP standards and adding CRC64-NVME support in 2019. Today, CRC64-NVME is now integrated into the...
item.dc:creator: wmarkley

item.title: Highlights from Git 2.55
item.description: The open source Git project just released Git 2.55. Here is GitHub’s look at some of the most interesting features and changes introduced since last time. The post Highlights from Git 2.55 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Packaging and Package Identity for .NET apps with WinApp CLI on Windows
item.description: Use the WinApp CLI to add identity to and package .NET desktop applications, enabling deeper integration to Windows and easier distribution. The post Packaging and Package Identity for .NET apps with WinApp CLI on Windows appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Teutsch

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now in preview for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now rolling out in preview on GitHub Copilot. Fast mode delivers significantly faster output token speeds while maintaining the same intelligence as Claude Opus… The post Claude Opus 4.8 (fast mode) is now in preview for GitHub Copilot appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Chromium extension uses AI‑related branding to redirect browser search
item.description: A malicious Chromium-based extension that spoofs the AI-powered answer engine Perplexity AI redirects browser search traffic using MV3 APIs and intermediary infrastructure. The post Chromium extension uses AI‑related branding to redirect browser search appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Microsoft Defender Experts

item.title: Inside the Advisory Database and what happens when vulnerability volume breaks records
item.description: The GitHub Advisory Database is processing more vulnerability reports than ever before. Here's what's driving the surge, how we're responding, and how the community can help. The post Inside the Advisory Database and what happens when vulnerability volume breaks records appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Your readiness playbook: adoption helper, costs, APIs, and the checklist
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl The transition is not a project—it is a sequence of small, ordered decisions. Here is the readiness tool that scores your environment in minutes, the cost story you can take to finance, the API strategy that future-proofs your...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1
